rules.description: <button> with no accessible name. Add text content, aria-label or aria-labelledby so screen-reader users hear what the button does.
Heading hierarchy skips a level (e.g. h1 followed by h3 with no h2). Screen-reader users rely on outline depth; don't skip.
element without an alt attribute. Screen readers will narrate the filename. Use alt="" for decorative images, otherwise describe the image.
<a> with no accessible name. Icon-only or empty anchors need aria-label, visible text, or a labelled child image/svg.
Body-level text width must sit inside the readable 55–75ch band (tolerance 45–85) at the primary breakpoint. Only fires on body/main/article/content selectors.
The canonical 44px gradient-fill + trailing arrow CTA is the template button.
The Product / Company / Resources / Legal footer is reflexive.
A single line-height across display, body and caption reads as untuned type.
Fade-up on everything with no stagger reason is decoration.
The centred pill + headline + subhead + two CTAs is the median landing page.
Ban the ten most-defaulted-to grotesques as the only font on the page.
Stock 3D hero blobs and default Spline scenes are visual fillers.

Em-dashes in prose (U+2014). The single most recognizable AI-writing tell. Rewrite with commas, colons, periods, parentheses or middle dots as each sentence demands. En-dashes (–) are fine for ranges.
A <li> or list paragraph that opens with an emoji is a slop tell.
Stock-avatar services betray fabricated testimonials.
A 'Trusted by' strip whose logos are not declared customers reads as theatre.
Pure-black background plus a single high-chroma accent is the stock dark-mode slop.
Gradient text on bait words (AI, future, tomorrow) is the canonical overreach.
Glassmorphism on more than one distinct component type is decoration, not design.
Inline style="animation:..." or style="transition:..." attributes on elements. Motion belongs in stylesheets where reduced-motion can be respected.
Lucide icon in a rounded-square gradient tile is the feature-card canonical.
Detect purple/blue/pink gradient triangles in raw CSS or Tailwind classes.
The silver diagonal sweep used on any non-loading surface is the AI shimmer tell.
Banned marketing phrases that signal median SaaS copy.
A row of exactly three similar cards is the canonical feature-section slop.
A page where every interactive surface shares one medium radius (rounded-xl / 2xl / 12–16px) reads as shadcn default.
Three pricing tiers with a 'Most Popular' middle is the SaaS cliché.
Buttons, cards and media sharing one medium radius flattens hierarchy. Sharp-only or sharp + pill are deliberate decisions and don't fire.
A page with no CSS grid or explicit column structure usually means flex-stacked slop.
A page needs at least two distinct font families.
Animations declared without a prefers-reduced-motion escape hatch.
One shadow on every card is a token fingerprint, not a shadow system.
The document is a JS-rendered SPA shell. Source lint cannot score design that is produced at runtime.
A perfectly mirrored composition is the stock-illustration / Alegria default. Tokens require at least one asymmetric element.
Monoline illustration (every stroke the same width) is a Feather/Lucide default tell.
Every colour used in the SVG should trace back to the token's declared palette.
No negative tracking on display type, no opened tracking on all-caps labels.
A page with only two font-weights (typically 400 and 600) lacks typographic voice.
Internal link whose target does not exist in the linted file set. AI writers hallucinate navigation; this catches links pointing at pages that were never built.
Bento grids need a cell of visual dominance.
Monoline Feather/Lucide icons with uniform 1.5px stroke fire info.
Fake cursive or unreadable script lettering overlaid on renders is a slop tell.
Image-generation tells: six-finger hands, twisted limbs, doubled teeth, merged fingers, extra joints.
Impossibly symmetrical, glossy, age-smoothed human faces are a generator fingerprint.
Generic 'diverse team of five smiling professionals' casting pattern is a stock / Corporate-Memphis hand-me-down.

A two-column section where one column is significantly taller than the other produces visible dead space in the shorter neighbour. Happens most commonly when a left column of text sits beside a right column of product cards that grow over time.
Mesh gradient backdrops require a typographic counter-force.
AI-rendered illustration with subsurface-scatter glow is forbidden.
Corporate Memphis illustration style is forbidden.
3D hero blobs with iridescent shader are forbidden.
Stock photo of a diverse team at a laptop is forbidden.
Page composition must not be perfectly symmetrical.
Lowercase grotesque + dot wordmark is a Y Combinator cliché.
Body text should be at least 14px at mobile widths. Below that, one-handed reading becomes work.
Content extends past the right edge of a 375px viewport. Catches unwrapped nav, overflowing pre blocks, fixed-width tables, and display-size headlines that don't scale with the viewport.

A horizontally-scrollable region hides its scrollbar without a replacement cue. Add scroll-snap, an edge-fade mask, or a data-scroll-affordance opt-out so touch users can see that more content exists.
Interactive elements should be at least 32px tall at mobile widths. Targets below this trip the fat-finger threshold and tank one-handed usability.
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, ..."> must be present. Without it, mobile browsers render the page at desktop width and scale down, defeating every other mobile treatment.
